title: Acoustics for Rehearsal Room
openingLine: Hi, this is Greg at Avon High. I just got back from a doctor's appointment and they told me I'm starting to lose hearing. I think part of it is the band room — it's just brutal in there. Can someone come out and look at it for acoustic treatment?
customerContext: You're 47 and your audiologist just confirmed early-onset hearing loss. You're scared, frustrated, and emotional about it. The band room has always been loud and you've shrugged it off, but now it feels personal. You don't have funding lined up — you're calling because you don't know who else to call. You need someone who takes you seriously.

hiddenInfo.reveal: I've been a band director for 22 years. This is the first time I've worried about my own health from the room.
Roughly 40x50 ft with a 16 ft ceiling. Concrete block walls, painted, no treatment.
Nothing allocated. Principal said 'bring me a number and I'll see what I can do.'
I'd love something done before next school year — that's six months out.
Not working with anyone. The district has facilities people but no one with acoustic background.
Principal can approve up to $25K. Anything more goes to the district.
We have Wenger chairs and stands but never any acoustic products.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Requested layout from facilities or offered field measurement
Researched purchase history before the visit
Asked about needs, budget, and timeline up front
Asked for customer expectations and what success looks like
Asked if they're working with a consultant or architect

Solution Framing.behavior: Discussed national contracts vs. competitive bidding
Offered help if the project is over budget (phasing, alternates)

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Probed for additional space needs beyond the band room

BANT.behavior: Discussed budget tied to solution effectiveness, not just price
Identified principal and district as decision makers

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Set follow-up tasks tied to the start-of-school deadline
